questions.question: Why does RLHF use pairwise preferences instead of a hand-written reward function?
options: "Helpfulness" has no closed-form expression over tokens — humans can't WRITE the reward but can cheaply COMPARE two responses and pick the better one; a reward model fitted to those comparisons supplies the scalar signal
questions.explanation: Christiano 2017 → InstructGPT 2022: the SFT → RM → PPO recipe is what turned GPT-3 into ChatGPT and shipped every aligned LLM of 2023-2025.

questions.question: How is the reward model trained?
options: Bradley-Terry pairwise logistic loss: −E[log σ(R(x,y₊) − R(x,y₋))] — the reward DIFFERENCE implies log-odds of preference; R is usually the SFT model with a scalar head on top
questions.explanation: Bradley-Terry dates to 1952 and remains the dominant choice. Same transformer backbone as the policy, one linear layer outputting the score.

questions.question: Why is the KL penalty "the single most important knob in RLHF"?
options: The RM was only trained on in-distribution completions — without the KL term (r = R_φ − β·KL(π_θ||π_ref)), PPO finds out-of-distribution reward hacks that score higher than any human-written response; the KL keeps the policy on the manifold where the RM is valid
questions.explanation: It's a regularizer (β ≈ 0.01-0.05), not a hard trust region — anchoring π_θ to the frozen π_SFT reference so reward optimization can't wander into the RM's blind spots.

questions.question: What replaced or extended the PPO stage by 2026?
options: DPO collapses RM + PPO into one supervised loss over preferences (cheaper, near-equal alignment quality); GRPO swaps the critic for a group-relative baseline with VERIFIER rewards (dominant for reasoning); PRMs score individual reasoning steps; RLAIF generates preferences with an LLM
Everything moved back to SFT only
questions.explanation: The reward-model concept survives everywhere even where PPO doesn't: Best-of-N sampling, RL-from-verifiable-rewards, and process reward models all rest on it.

questions.question: What does each of the three RLHF stages contribute?
options: SFT biases the base model toward demonstrated good behavior; the RM converts cheap human comparisons into a scalar reward; PPO with KL then pushes the policy toward higher reward without leaving the SFT distribution
questions.explanation: A pipeline of progressively cheaper signals: expensive demonstrations → cheaper comparisons → automated reward queries. Note PPO advantages are token-level but the RM scores only the full response.
